author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter IX.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have just read the long lesson of the man born blind, whom the Lord Jesus restored to the light; but were we to attempt handling the whole of it, and considering, according to our ability, each passage in a way proportionate to its worth, the day would be insufficient.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore I ask and warn your Charity not to require any words of ours on those passages whose meaning is manifest; for it would be too protracted to linger at each.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I proceed, therefore, to set forth briefly the mystery of this blind man’s enlightenment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All, certainly, that was done by our Lord Jesus Christ, both works and words, are worthy of our astonishment and admiration: His works, because they are facts; His words, because they are signs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we reflect, then, on what is signified by the deed here done, that blind man is the human race; for this blindness had place in the first man, through sin, from whom we all draw our origin, not only in respect of death, but also of unrighteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if unbelief is blindness, and faith enlightenment, whom did Christ find a believer at His coming? seeing that the apostle, belonging himself to the family of the prophets, says: “And we also in times past were by nature the children of wrath, even as others.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If “children of wrath,” then children of vengeance, children of punishment, children of hell.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how is it “by nature,” save that through the first man sinning moral evil rooted itself in us as a nature?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If evil has so taken root within us, every man is born mentally blind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he sees, he has no need of a guide.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If he does need one to guide and enlighten him, then is he blind from his birth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord came: what did He do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He set forth a great mystery.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He spat on the ground,” He made clay of His spittle; for the Word was made flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And He anointed the eyes of the blind man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The anointing had taken place, and yet he saw not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He sent him to the pool which is called Siloam.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it was the evangelist’s concern to call our attention to the name of this pool; and he adds, “Which is interpreted, Sent.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You understand now who it is that was sent; for had He not been sent, none of us would have been set free from iniquity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly he washed his eyes in that pool which is interpreted, Sent—he was baptized in Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, therefore, when He baptized him in a manner in Himself, He then enlightened him; when He anointed Him, perhaps He made him a catechumen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In many different ways indeed may the profound meaning of such a sacramental act be set forth and handled; but let this suffice your Charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have heard a great mystery.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask a man, Are you a Christian?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His answer to you is, I am not, if he is a pagan or a Jew.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if he says, I am; you inquire again of him, Are you a catechumen or a believer?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If he reply, A catechumen; he has been anointed, but not yet washed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how anointed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Inquire, and he will answer you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Inquire of him in whom he believes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that very respect in which he is a catechumen he says, In Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, I am speaking in a way both to the faithful and to catechumens.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What have I said of the spittle and the clay?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That the Word was made flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This even catechumens hear; but that to which they have been anointed is not all they need; let them hasten to the font if they are in search of enlightenment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now, because of certain points in the lesson before us, let us run over the words of the Lord, and of the whole lesson itself rather than make them a theme of discourse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “As He passed out, He saw a man who was blind;” blind, not from any cause whatever, but “from his birth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And His disciples asked Him, Rabbi.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know that “Rabbi” is Master.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They called Him Master, because they desired to learn.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The question, at all events, they proposed to the Lord as a master, “Who did sin, this man, or his parents, that he was born blind?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus answered, “Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents,” that he was born blind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this that He has said?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If no man is sinless, were the parents of this blind man without sin?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was he himself either born without original sin, or had he committed none in the course of his lifetime?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because his eyes were closed, had his lusts lost their wakefulness?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How many evils are done by the blind?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From what evil does an evil mind abstain, even though the eyes are closed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He could not see, but he knew how to think, and perchance to lust after something which his blindness hindered him from attaining, and so still in his heart to be judged by the searcher of hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, both his parents had sin, and the man himself had sin, wherefore said the Lord, “Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents,” but only in respect to the point on which he was questioned, “that he was born blind”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For his parents had sin; but not by reason of the sin itself did it come about that he was born blind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, it was not through the parents’ sin that he was born blind, why was he born blind?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the Master as He teaches.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He seeks one who believes, to give him understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He Himself tells us the reason why that man was born blind: “Neither hath this man sinned,” He says, “nor his parents: but that the works of God should be made manifest in him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then, what follows?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I must work the works of Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, here is that sent one [Siloam], wherein the blind man washed his face.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And see what He said: “I must work the works of Him that sent me, while it is day.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Recall to thy mind the way in which He gives universal glory to Him of whom He is: for that One has the Son who is of Him; He Himself has no One of whom He is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore, Lord, saidst Thou, “While it is day”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hearken why He did so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The night cometh when no man can work.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not even Thou, Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Will that night have such power that not even Thou, whose work the night is, wilt be able to work therein?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I think, Lord Jesus, nay I do not think, but believe and hold it sure, that Thou wast there when God said, “Let there be light, and there was light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He made it by the Word, He made it by Thee: and therefore it is said, “All things were made by Him; and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “God divided between the light and the darkness: the light He called Day, and the darkness He called Night.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is that night wherein, when it comes, no one shall be able to work?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what the day is, and then thou wilt understand what the night is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how shall we hear what the day is?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let Himself tell us: “As long as I am in this world, I am the light of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, He Himself is the day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the blind man wash his eyes in the day, that he may behold the day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “As long,” He says, “as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then will it be night of a kind unknown to me, when Christ will no longer be there; and so no one will be able to work.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: An inquiry remains, my brethren; patiently listen to me as I inquire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With you I inquire: With you shall I find Him to whom my inquiry is addressed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are agreed; for it is expressly and definitely stated that the Lord proclaimed Himself in this place as the day, that is, the light of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “As long,” He says, “as I am in this world, I am the light of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He Himself works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how long is He in this world?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are we to think, brethren, that He was here then, and is here no longer?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we think so, then already, after the Lord’s ascension, did that fearful night begin, when no one can work.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If that night began after the Lord’s ascension, how was it that the apostles wrought so much?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was that the night when the Holy Spirit came, and, filling all who were in one place, gave them the power of speaking in the tongues of every nation?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it night when that lame man was made whole at the word of Peter, or rather, at the word of the Lord dwelling in Peter?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it night when, as the disciples were passing by, the sick were laid in couches, that they might be touched at least by their shadow as they passed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet, when the Lord was here, there was no one made whole by His shadow as He passed; but He Himself had said to the disciples, “Greater things than these shall ye do.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yes, the Lord had said, “Greater things than these shall ye do;” but let not flesh and blood exalt itself: let such hear Him also saying, “Without me ye can do nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall we say of that night?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When will it be, when no one shall be able to work?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It will be that night of the wicked, that night of those to whom it shall be said in the end, “Depart into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it is here called night, not flame, nor fire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hearken, then, why it is also night.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of a certain servant He says, “Bind ye him hand and foot, and cast him into outer darkness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let man, then, work while he liveth, that he may not be overtaken by that night when no man can work.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is now that faith is working by love; and if now we are working, then this is the day—Christ is here.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear His promise, and think Him not absent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is Himself who hath said, “Lo, I am with you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let there be no anxiety in us who are alive; were it possible, with this very word we might place in perfect security the generations still to come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Lo,” He says,” I am with you always, even to the end of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That day, which is completed by the circuit of yonder sun, has but few hours; the day of Christ’s presence extends even to the end of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But after the resurrection of the living and the dead, when He shall say to those placed at His right hand, “Come, ye blessed of my Father, receive the kingdom;” and to those at His left, “Depart into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels;” then shall be the night when no man can work, but only get back what he has wrought before.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is a time for working, another for receiving; for the Lord shall render to every one according to his works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: While thou livest, be doing, if thou art to be doing at all; for then shall come that appalling night, to envelope the wicked in its folds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But even now every unbeliever, when he dies, is received within that night: there is no work to be done there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that night was the rich man burning, and asking a drop of water from the beggar’s finger; he mourned, agonized, confessed, but no relief was vouchsafed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He even endeavored to do good; for he said to Abraham, “Father Abraham, send Lazarus to my brethren, that he may tell them what is being done here, lest they also come into this place of torment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Unhappy man! when thou wert living, then was the time for working: now thou art already in the night, in which no man can work.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “When He had thus spoken, He spat on the ground, and made clay of the spittle, and He spread the clay upon his eyes, and said unto him, Go and wash in the pool of Siloam (which is, by interpretation, Sent).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He went his way therefore, and washed, and came seeing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As these words are clear, we may pass them over.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The neighbors therefore, and those who saw him previously, for he was a beggar, said, Is not this he who sat and begged?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some said, It is he: others, No; but he is like him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The opening of his eyes had altered his countenance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He said, I am he.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His voice utters its gratitude, that it might not be condemned as ungrateful.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore said they unto him, How were thine eyes opened?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He answered, The man who is called Jesus made clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said unto me, Go to the pool of Siloam, and wash: and I went and washed, and saw.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, he is become the herald of grace; see, he preaches the gospel; endowed with sight, he becomes a confessor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That blind man makes confession, and the heart of the wicked was troubled; for they had not in their heart what he had now in his countenance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They said to him, Where is he who hath opened thine eyes?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said, I know not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In these words the man’s own soul was like that of one only as yet anointed, but not yet seeing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us so put it, brethren, as if he had that anointing in his soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He preaches, and knows not the Being whom he preaches.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They brought to the Pharisees him who had been blind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And it was the Sabbath when Jesus made the clay, and opened his eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then again the Pharisees also asked how he had received his sight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he said unto them, He put clay upon mine eyes, and I washed, and do see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore said some of the Pharisees;” not all, but some; for some were already anointed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then said those who neither saw nor were anointed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This man is not of God, because he keepeth not the Sabbath.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He it was rather who kept it, who was without sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is the spiritual Sabbath, to have no sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In fact, brethren, it is of this that God admonishes us, when He commends the Sabbath to our notice: “Thou shalt do no servile work.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These are God’s words when commending the Sabbath, “Thou shalt do no servile work.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now ask the former lessons, what is meant by servile work; and listen to the Lord: “Every one that committeth sin is the servant of sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But these men, neither seeing, as I said, nor anointed, kept the Sabbath carnally, and profaned it spiritually.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Others said, How can a man that is a sinner do such miracles?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These were the anointed ones.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And there was a division among them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The day had divided between the light and the darkness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They say then unto the blind man again, What sayest thou of him who hath opened thine eyes?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is thy feeling about him? what is thine opinion? what is thy judgment?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They sought how to revile the man, that he might be cast out of the synagogue, but be found by Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he steadfastly expressed what he felt.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he said, “That he is a prophet.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As yet, indeed, anointed only in heart, he does not thus far confess the Son of God, and yet he speaks not untruthfully.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord saith of Himself, “A prophet is not without honor, save in his own country.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore the Jews did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and received his sight, till they called the parents of him that received his sight;” that is, who had been blind, and had come to the possession of sight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And they asked them, saying, Is this your son, who ye say was born blind? how then doth he now see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His parents answered them, and said, We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind: but how he now seeth, we know not; or who hath opened his eyes, we know not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they said, Ask himself; he is of age, let him speak of himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is indeed our son, and we might justly be compelled to answer for him as an infant, because then he could not speak for himself: from of old he has had power of speech, only now he sees: we have been acquainted with him as blind from his birth, we know him as having speech from of old, only now do we see him endowed with sight: ask himself, that you may be instructed; why seek to calumniate us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “These words spake his parents, because they feared the Jews: for the Jews had conspired already, that if any man did confess that He was Christ, he should be put out of the synagogue.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was no longer a bad thing to be put out of the synagogue.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They cast out, but Christ received.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore said his parents, He is of age, ask himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then again called they the man who had been blind, and said unto him, Give God the glory.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is that, “Give God the glory”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Deny what thou hast received.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such conduct is manifestly not to give God the glory, but rather to blaspheme Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Give God,” they say, “the glory: we know that this man is a sinner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then said he, If he is a sinner, I know not: one thing I know, that whereas I was blind, now I see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then said they to him, What did he to thee, how opened he thine eyes?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he, indignant now at the hardness of the Jews, and as one brought from a state of blindness to sight, unable to endure the blind, “answered them, I have told you already, and ye have heard: wherefore would ye hear it again?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Will ye also become his disciples?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means, “Will ye also,” but that I am one already?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Will ye also be so?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now I see, but see not askance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They cursed him, and said, Thou art his disciple.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such a malediction be upon us, and upon our children!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For a malediction it is, if thou layest open their heart, not if thou ponderest the words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But we are Moses’ disciples.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We know that God spake unto Moses: as for this fellow, we know not from whence he is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Would ye had known that “God spake to Moses!” ye would have also known that God preached by Moses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For ye have the Lord saying, “Had ye believed Moses, ye would have also believed me; for he wrote of me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it thus ye follow the servant, and turn your back against the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But not even the servant do ye follow; for by him ye would be guided to the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The man answered and said unto them, Herein is a marvellous thing, that ye know not from whence he is, and yet he hath opened mine eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now we know that God heareth not sinners; but if any man is a worshipper of God, and doeth His will, him He heareth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He speaks still as one only anointed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For God heareth even sinners.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if God heard not sinners, in vain would the publican, casting his eyes on the ground, and smiting on his breast, have said, “Lord, be merciful to me a sinner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that confession merited justification, as this blind man enlightenment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If this man were not of God, he could do nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With frankness, constancy, and truthfulness [he spoke].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For these things that were done by the Lord, by whom were they done but by God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or when would such things be done by disciples, were not the Lord dwelling in them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They answered and said unto him, Thou wast wholly born in sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means this “wholly”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even to blindness of the eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He who has opened his eyes, also saves him wholly: He will grant a resurrection at His right hand, who gave enlight enment to his countenance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Thou wast altogether born in sins, and dost thou teach us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they cast him out.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They had made him their master; many questions had they asked for their own instruction, and they ungratefully cast forth their teacher.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, as I have already said before, brethren, when they expel, the Lord receiveth; for the rather that he was expelled, was he made a Christian.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when He had found him, He said unto him, Dost thou believe on the Son of God?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now He washes the face of his heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He answered and said,” as one still only anointed, “Who is he, Lord, that I might believe on him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Jesus said unto him, Thou hast both seen Him, and it is He that talketh with thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The One is He that is sent; the other is one washing his face in Siloam, which is interpreted, Sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now at last, with the face of his heart washed, and a conscience purified, acknowledging Him not only as the son of man, which he had believed before, but now as the Son of God, who had assumed our flesh, “he said, Lord, I believe.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is but little to say, “I believe:” wouldst thou also see what he believes Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He fell down and worshipped Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And Jesus said to him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now is He, the day, discerning between the light and the darkness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For judgment am I come into this world; that they who see not might see, and they who see might be made blind.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A weighty subject of inquiry hast Thou laid on the weary; but revive our strength that we may be able to understand what Thou hast said.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art come “that they who see not may see:” rightly so, for Thou art the light: rightly so, for Thou art the day: rightly so, for Thou deliverest from darkness: this every soul accepts, every one understands.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this that follows, “And those who see may be made blind?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shall then, because Thou art come, those be made blind who saw?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what follows, and perhaps thou wilt understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By these words, then, were “some of the Pharisees” disturbed, “and said unto Him, Are we blind also?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear now what it is that moved them, “And they who see may be made blind.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus said unto them, If ye were blind, ye should have no sin;” while blindness itself is sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If ye were blind,” that is, if ye considered yourselves blind, if ye called yourselves blind, ye also would have recourse to the physician: “if” then in this way “ye were blind, ye should have no sin;” for I am come to take away sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But now ye say, We see; [therefore] your sin remaineth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because by saying, “We see:” ye seek not the physician, ye remain in your blindness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, then, is that which a little above we did not understand, when He said, “I am come, that they who see not may see;” for what means this, “that they who see not may see”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who acknowledge that they do not see, and seek the physician, that they may receive sight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And “they who see may be made blind:” what means this, “they who see may be made blind”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That they who think they see, and seek not the physician, may abide in their blindness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such discerning therefore of one from another He called judgment, when He said, “For judgment I am come into this world,” whereby He distinguishes the cause of those who believe and make confession from the proud, who think they see, and are therefore the more grievously blinded: just as the sinner, making confession, and seeking the physician, said to Him, “Judge me, O God, and discern my cause against the unholy nation,”—namely, those who say, “We see,” and their sin remaineth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it was not that judgment He now brought into the world, whereby in the end of the world He shall judge the living and the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in respect to this He had said, “I judge no man;” seeing that He came the first time, “not to judge the world, but that the world through Him might be saved.”
